sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.33 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: (A.\,Bauer). Does Higman's Embedding Theorem relativize in the following way? Is it the case that for every subset$X$, there is a finitely generated group$G_X$that has an$X$-computable presentation (that is, there is a finite generating set relative to which the set of relations is computably enumerable with an$X$oracle), and such that any finitely generated group has an$X$-computable presentation...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
